Adapt

What do you think? …

Chris Messina
Raycast

Raycast

It's SETI at Home but for inference! 🤩

Gabriel P.
Features.Vote

Features.Vote [...] Gabriel P.
Features.Vote

Features.Vote

the auto-configured p2p setup is the clever bit. most self-hosted inference solutions require you to manually manage which node is running what model, handle routing yourself, and accept that you'll be ssh-ing into machines whenever something needs updating. auto-configuring the mesh and exposing a standard openai-compatible endpoint means your existing agent tooling just works without a custom client. [...] where this gets hard is reliability under real agent workflows. spare capacity is honest framing, but spare capacity is also the most volatile kind. when an agent mid-task makes a follow-up call and the node has dropped or a worker left the mesh, the retry behavior matters a lot. handling partial failures gracefully without surfacing errors to whatever client is consuming the api is a non-trivial coordination problem, especially as the mesh grows beyond a few trusted machines.

•4 reviews

I’ve been seeing tons of creative tutorials on Rednote about Deepseek lately — people are asking it to be their daily fitness coach or even using it to "read their fortune." I love how AI tools like this are becoming everyday friends in such playful ways. Over the past few months, I’ve been experimenting with Deepseek myself — sending it well-organized materials and asking it to edit based on my logic. For longer articles, I still prefer the results from ChatGPT, but when it comes to quick career advice, resume tips, or professional insights, I find Deepseek's answers surprisingly helpful.
